title: bin/init.js entrypoint + CLAUDE.md first-chat routing for project intake
description:
Third ticket in the project-intake wizard chain (TASK-010..015). Wires the engine (TASK-010) + the question library/PROJECT.md writer (TASK-011) into a working CLI that a non-technical user can run after cloning the framework. Also amends CLAUDE.md so the Orchestrator detects uninitialized repos on the first chat and routes the human to the wizard.

Deliverables:

1. **`bin/init.js`** — Entrypoint CLI. Calls `runQuestionnaire(buildIntakeQuestions())` with a real `node:readline/promises` prompter and a `persistTo` path inside the active session bundle. On completion, calls `writeProjectMd({repoRoot, answers, now})`. Prints a friendly final message pointing the user to the next step (start a chat with the orchestrator).

2. **State machine** for the entrypoint, exactly three branches:
   - **PROJECT.md exists** → print a one-paragraph summary (name, type, created_at) and exit 0. No mutation. Unless `--force` is passed, in which case continue to the wizard and overwrite on completion.
   - **intake.json exists in the active session bundle AND PROJECT.md does NOT exist** → auto-resume the wizard from the engine's persisted `lastAnsweredId`. Friendly message tells the user we're resuming.
   - **Neither** → fresh start. Create a new session bundle via `startSession` from `src/lifecycle.js`, then run the wizard with `persistTo` set to `state/sessions/<new-id>/intake.json`.

3. **`runInit({argv, prompter, repoRoot, now, hostname})`** — exported testable function. The file's top-level executable path calls `runInit({argv: process.argv.slice(2), prompter: realReadlinePrompter, repoRoot: process.cwd(), now: () => new Date().toISOString(), hostname: os.hostname()})`. Same `pathToFileURL` Windows-safe gate as `bin/new-task.js`.

4. **CLAUDE.md amendment.** Add a new section at the very top of the file (ABOVE 'RESUME FIRST') titled 'First-chat routing'. Content: 'Before reading the RESUME FIRST section: if `PROJECT.md` does not exist in the repo root, the framework has not been initialized for this project. Direct the human to run `node bin/init.js` (the project intake wizard) before any other workflow step.'

5. **No new external deps.** Reuse `node:readline/promises` for the real prompter (same as `bin/new-task.js`). Reuse `startSession` and the pointer/bundle writers from `src/lifecycle.js`. Reuse `runQuestionnaire`, `buildIntakeQuestions`, and `writeProjectMd` from prior tickets.
acceptance_criteria: `bin/init.js` exports `runInit({argv, prompter, repoRoot, now, hostname})` returning `Promise<{state, projectMdPath, sessionId}>` where `state` is one of 'created' | 'already_initialized' | 'resumed' | 'forced'.

PROJECT.md-present branch: when PROJECT.md exists at `<repoRoot>/PROJECT.md`, `runInit({argv: []})` returns `{state: 'already_initialized'}` without calling the prompter, without writing any file, and prints a summary line containing the project name and type.
PROJECT.md-present + `--force`: when PROJECT.md exists and `argv` contains `--force`, the wizard runs to completion and overwrites PROJECT.md. Returns `{state: 'forced'}`.

intake-resume branch: when `state/sessions/<active>/intake.json` exists with a valid `{answers, lastAnsweredId}` payload AND PROJECT.md does NOT exist, `runInit` resumes the wizard — prompter is only called for questions whose ids are absent from the loaded `answers`. Returns `{state: 'resumed'}`.

fresh-start branch: when neither PROJECT.md nor an active intake.json exists, `runInit` calls `startSession` to create a new bundle, then runs the wizard with `persistTo` set to that bundle's `intake.json`, then writes PROJECT.md. Returns `{state: 'created', sessionId: <new bundle id>}`.

CLAUDE.md has a new section titled 'First-chat routing' as its first heading-level-2 section (immediately after the document title, BEFORE 'RESUME FIRST'). The section instructs the orchestrator to direct the human to `node bin/init.js` when PROJECT.md is missing.

New `tests/init.spec.js` covers all four state branches (already_initialized, forced, resumed, created) using `tmpRepo` + fake prompter, asserts on disk state after each (PROJECT.md presence, bundle creation when appropriate, intake.json preservation).
Unknown flag in argv throws clearly with the flag name in the error message (parity with `bin/new-task.js`).
The existing 90 vitest specs continue to pass without modification — the new tests are additive.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created. Design fixed pre-tests: three-branch state machine (already_initialized / resumed / created / forced), CLI exports a testable `runInit({argv, prompter, repoRoot, now, hostname})` function so tests inject everything. Persistence lives inside the session bundle (state/sessions/<id>/intake.json) so a Ctrl+C in the middle of the wizard can be picked up on the next invocation without a manual flag. CLAUDE.md gets a new 'First-chat routing' section above RESUME FIRST so the orchestrator detects uninitialized repos before doing anything else. No --resume flag — auto-resume is the right default UX for non-technical users. Workflow: developer writes failing Vitest suite covering the 4 state-machine branches + flag parsing + CLAUDE.md routing check, then implements, then reviewer in fresh context.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body:
Closed. Workflow: tests-first commit `67f342e` (8 failing specs in tests/init.spec.js + tests/helpers/scripted-prompter.js helper; phase-1 dev surfaced a real signature gap that startSession doesn't accept a hostname injection) + impl commit `5de16a9` (bin/init.js with the four-branch state machine, real readline prompter that handles enum/multi/string types, CLAUDE.md amendment placing '## First-chat routing' between the title and RESUME FIRST; impl commit body documents the hostname-dropped rationale, branch-ordering rationale, and the `*` marker decision per the discipline established in TASK-011). Reviewer in fresh context returned **APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS** (0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, 1 LOW, 2 NIT). 98/98 vitest specs green across two runs (~4.2s each), deterministic.

Orchestrator decision documented in impl commit: dropped `hostname` from runInit's contract entirely after phase-1 dev correctly flagged that `startSession` calls `hostFingerprint()` directly via `os.hostname()` — extending startSession with a hostname injection point was scope creep, and the test fixtures don't assert on hostname anyway. JS silently ignores the extra `hostname` key passed by tests.

AC coverage map: AC1 (runInit signature + return shape) — every test asserts on `{state, projectMdPath, sessionId}`; AC2 (already_initialized mutation-free) — `already_initialized_when_project_md_exists` (throwIfCalled prompter); AC3 (--force overwrites) — `forced_overwrites_existing_project_md`; AC4 (resume from partial intake, same bundle id) — `resumed_from_partial_intake`; AC5 (fresh-start creates new session) — `created_from_empty_repo`; AC6 (CLAUDE.md routing placement + body) — `claude_md_has_first_chat_routing_above_resume_first`; AC7 (tests cover all four branches) — confirmed by reviewer's branch-by-branch trace; AC8 (unknown flag throws with name) — `unknown_flag_throws_with_flag_name`; AC9 (no regression on 90 pre-existing) — 98/98 vs 90/90 baseline.

Reviewer finding rolled into TASK-015 (UX polish): the 1 LOW (positional tokens silently ignored by `bin/init.js`'s argv parser — `bin/new-task.js` throws on any unrecognized token regardless of `--` prefix; a typo like `node bin/init.js froce` would silently fall through). Same UX polish pass that fixes the TASK-010 dead-code can drop the `tok.startsWith('--')` guard for argv parity. The 2 NITs (already_initialized summary line not prefixed with `*`, realReadlinePrompter doesn't close its readline interface — pre-existing pattern from bin/new-task.js, parity confirmed) noted in-line, no follow-up needed.

Wizard chain progress after this close: TASK-010 done (engine), TASK-011 done (library + PROJECT.md), TASK-012 done (init + routing), TASK-013 next but blocked by TASK-016 (PROJECT.md hardening — gating per TASK-011 reviewer), TASK-014 + TASK-015 still queued. Non-chain open: TASK-003 (Jira mig, blocked on MCP creds), TASK-005 (npm audit), TASK-008 (TASK-004 polish), TASK-009 (task-store hardening), TASK-016 (PROJECT.md hardening).
